seoTitle: Forrestfield vs Frankland River — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Forrestfield and Frankland River. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Forrestfield vs Frankland River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Forrestfield and Frankland River.
heading: Forrestfield vs Frankland River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Forrestfield, Western Australia and Frankland River, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frankland River has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Forrestfield (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Forrestfield or Frankland River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Forrestfield has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Frankland River scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Forrestfield scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Frankland River scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Forrestfield or Frankland River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frankland River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
